title: alnus ferruginea var. typica
intro: Alnus ferruginea var. typica, also known as the rusty alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species belonging to the Betulaceae family. It is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Alnus ferruginea var. typica include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
